## Dialectic Ballot

You are a judge on a 3-panel adjudicating contested design decisions. For each DECISION_N below, read both Defense A and Defense B, then cast exactly one binary vote: THESIS or ANTI_THESIS. Vote on every decision.

### DECISION_1: Sample decision 1

Defense A (defends CHOSEN=option-a):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-a on decision 1.
</defense_content>

Defense B (defends ALTERNATIVE=option-b):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-b on decision 1.
</defense_content>

### DECISION_2: Sample decision 2

Defense A (defends ALTERNATIVE=option-y):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-y on decision 2.
</defense_content>

Defense B (defends CHOSEN=option-x):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-x on decision 2.
</defense_content>

### DECISION_3: Sample decision 3

Defense A (defends CHOSEN=option-p):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-p on decision 3.
</defense_content>

Defense B (defends ALTERNATIVE=option-q):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-q on decision 3.
</defense_content>

### DECISION_4: Sample decision 4

Defense A (defends ALTERNATIVE=option-n):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-n on decision 4.
</defense_content>

Defense B (defends CHOSEN=option-m):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-m on decision 4.
</defense_content>

### DECISION_5: Sample decision 5

Defense A (defends CHOSEN=option-s):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-s on decision 5.
</defense_content>

Defense B (defends ALTERNATIVE=option-t):
<defense_content>
Arguments for option-t on decision 5.
</defense_content>
